pi_items.source: Web search confirmed cholera is part of IDSR (Integrated Disease Surveillance and Response) in Sierra Leone, tracked as a priority disease with 26 reportable diseases in DHIS2 system.

DHIS2 official documentation (https://docs.dhis2.org/en/implement/database-design/aggregate-system-design/data-sets-and-forms.html) confirms Weekly as a standard period type option. Sierra Leone confirmed to use weekly IDSR reporting (https://wwwnc.cdc.gov/eid/article/28/13/22-0711_article).
Task requirement specification. No contradictory evidence found regarding minimum data elements configuration in DHIS2 datasets.

Web search confirmed Kenema is a district in Sierra Leone. DHIS2 implementation in Sierra Leone specifically mentioned piloting in Kenema district (https://play.dhis2.org/demo). Kenema District would exist as an organisation unit in Sierra Leone demo database.

Web search confirmed: (1) Cholera is part of IDSR surveillance in Sierra Leone; (2) Diarrhoea is documented as a disease tracked in DHIS2 (https://www.who.int/news-room/fact-sheets/detail/diarrhoeal-disease); (3) ORS (Oral Rehydration Solution) is standard treatment for diarrhoea per WHO; (4) Cases and deaths are documented as tracked data in DHIS2 surveillance systems; (5) Dysentery and dehydration are standard disease surveillance parameters. No contradictions found for any keyword.
Task description explicitly requires creating and configuring a DHIS2 Data Set, which is a metadata configuration task per DHIS2 design documentation.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the official Sierra Leone DHIS2 demo database (real-world health data from Sierra Leone's actual DHIS2 implementation). Kenema District is a confirmed real district in Sierra Leone that was piloted in the DHIS2 rollout. Weekly is a standard DHIS2 period type. Cholera, diarrhoea, dysentery, and death/cases data elements are all confirmed components of disease surveillance in Sierra Leone's DHIS2 system. The task requires creating a Data Set named 'Cholera Outbreak Surveillance - Kenema' with Weekly period type, assigning at least 3 data elements from relevant disease surveillance keywords, and assigning the dataset to Kenema organisation units.
